text.title: Spring Boot Patterns Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules for idiomatic Spring Boot patterns.

rule.statement: Every group of related properties MUST bind to a `@ConfigurationProperties` record/class. Direct `@Value` usage for >3 properties in one bean is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Type-safe binding catches misconfiguration at startup; @Value spreads brittle string references across the codebase.

rule.statement: REST APIs MUST translate business + validation exceptions to RFC 7807 ProblemDetail via a `@ControllerAdvice` extending `ResponseEntityExceptionHandler`.

rationale.source: RFC 7807; Spring Framework reference — exception handling

rule.rationale: Uniform error contract simplifies client code and observability dashboards.

rule.statement: Optional / feature-flag beans MUST be wired with `@ConditionalOnProperty(name=..., havingValue=...)`. Environment-specific beans MUST use `@Profile` not `if (env.equals(...))` checks.
rule.rationale: Conditions are evaluated at bootstrap; runtime if-branches drift and are missed by static analysis.

rule.statement: Every `@ConfigurationProperties` class MUST annotate `@Validated` and constrain each field with Jakarta Bean Validation (`@NotNull`, `@Min`, `@Pattern`).
rule.rationale: Misconfigured production deploys are caught at startup, not at first request.

rule.statement: Beans MUST NOT read configuration via `System.getenv()` / `System.getProperty()`. All config flows through `Environment` / `@Value` / `@ConfigurationProperties`.
rule.rationale: Direct env reads bypass profiles, validation, and test overrides; impossible to mock.
